userStories.title: Include currentTenant in GET /properties responses
userStories.description: As a landlord viewing the dossier, I want to see the active tenant's {id, name} on the property so the CHAT button and tenant cards work without a second API call.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/properties/:id returns `currentTenant: { id, name } | null` — populated when a RentalProcess with status='ACTIVE' exists for that property; null otherwise
GET /api/properties/search returns the same field per item (or document in PR notes if implementing via ?expand=tenant instead)
Query uses a Prisma `include` or join — no N+1. Verify by enabling Prisma query log in a test and asserting the number of queries is constant regardless of page size
Integration test: property without ACTIVE RentalProcess → currentTenant null; property with ACTIVE → currentTenant:{id,name}
Swagger Property schema exposes currentTenant as a nullable object with {id, name}
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Semantic decision: the PRD/BACKEND_HANDOFF reference 'RentalProcess.status=ACTIVE' but the `ProcessStatus` enum in this codebase (TRIAGE|VISIT_SCHEDULED|CONTRACT_ANALYSIS|CLOSED) has no ACTIVE value. The model that actually carries `status=ACTIVE` AND a `tenantId` is `Contract` (ContractStatus=ACTIVE|TERMINATED|COMPLETED). Existing `contractService.createContract` already sets `Property.status='RENTED'` in the same tx as the ACTIVE Contract insert, so Contract+Property are intended to track 'active occupancy'. Implementation therefore sources currentTenant from the most-recent ACTIVE Contract per Property via `include: { contracts: { where: { status: 'ACTIVE' }, select: { tenant: { select: { id, name } } }, take: 1, orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' } } }`. Three query paths updated: (a) `getPropertyById` — 1 query via Prisma include (JOIN); (b) `searchProperties` findMany branch — count + findMany-with-include = 2 queries constant; (c) `searchProperties` raw SQL (geolocation) branch — 1 extra batched `contract.findMany({ where: { status: 'ACTIVE', propertyId: { in: ids } } })` call, so 3 queries total constant regardless of page size. Swagger Property schema extended with nullable currentTenant { id, name }. PII scope deliberately minimal (no email/phone). New test tests/propertyCurrentTenant.test.ts mocks `src/config/db` Prisma client and covers: null when no contract, populated when contract, null when property missing, exact include shape (status=ACTIVE + tenant select), findMany path result shape + 2-query invariant at page size 10, raw SQL path result shape + single batched contract query, raw SQL path skips contract query when zero results. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit` → exit 0; `npx vitest run tests/propertyCurrentTenant.test.ts` → 8/8 pass; related test set (propertyCurrentTenant + propertyStatus + propertyPhotoUpload + seedUuidRegression + seed + generators + demoIds) → 66/66 pass; full suite 229 pass / 15 fail — exactly +8 vs. US-003 baseline (221/15), same pre-existing Auth0→Firebase gaps (auth.test.ts, userSync.test.ts, ingestKnowledge.test.ts). No regressions. No DB migration needed (Contract model already exists).

userStories.title: Auto-transition Property.status on RentalProcess lifecycle
userStories.description: As the system, I must keep Property.status consistent with RentalProcess lifecycle in the same transaction so the UI never shows 'contract active' + 'property available' simultaneously.
acceptanceCriteria: Wherever RentalProcess.status transitions to ACTIVE (service code — grep 'RentalProcess' in src/services/), Property.status is set to RENTED inside the SAME prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => ...) callback
Wherever a RentalProcess reaches a terminal state (end-of-term, early termination, cancellation — enumerate in code), Property.status is reset to AVAILABLE in the same transaction

Activating a second RentalProcess while another is already ACTIVE for the same property throws a clear error (e.g., code 'RENTAL_PROCESS_ALREADY_ACTIVE', status 409) and does NOT mutate state — the transaction rolls back
Integration test: activate RentalProcess → GET /properties/:id returns status:RENTED; terminate → status:AVAILABLE
Integration test: simulate tx.rollback after property status mutation (e.g., force createMany to fail) and assert Property.status is unchanged
Swagger docs note that Property.status may mutate as a side-effect of RentalProcess endpoints (a short description under the Property component schema is enough)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Semantic mapping up-front: the PRD references 'RentalProcess.status=ACTIVE' and 'terminal state' but the codebase's ProcessStatus enum (TRIAGE|VISIT_SCHEDULED|CONTRACT_ANALYSIS|CLOSED) has no ACTIVE value and the 'active occupancy' link is modeled on Contract (ContractStatus=ACTIVE|TERMINATED|COMPLETED). So the auto-transition logic lives across two services: (1) contractService.createContract already set Property.status='RENTED' inside a $transaction — added the 409 RENTAL_PROCESS_ALREADY_ACTIVE guard as the first step inside the same tx (tx.contract.findFirst on {propertyId, status:ACTIVE}) — any second-activation attempt throws before any write and the whole tx is rolled back; (2) new contractService.updateContractStatus(id, newStatus) runs inside $transaction: 404 CONTRACT_NOT_FOUND if missing; same-state no-op; ACTIVE → TERMINATED/COMPLETED flips Property.status to AVAILABLE in the same tx; terminal → ACTIVE re-checks the 409 guard and flips Property.status to RENTED; (3) rentalProcessService.updateStatus now wraps the Prisma update in $transaction — when newStatus=CLOSED AND property.status=NEGOTIATING (no contract yet), the same tx resets Property.status to AVAILABLE. RENTED stays untouched (contract termination is the authoritative path). New PATCH /api/contracts/:id/status route + controller.updateStatus wired into existing contractRoutes mounted with authStack at /api. Swagger Property.status.description notes the lifecycle side-effects. Tests: tests/rentalLifecycle.test.ts (new, 13 cases) mocks prisma with a $transaction wrapper that proxies to the same model mocks, covers: activation sets RENTED and calls $transaction once; 409 guard when ACTIVE contract exists (no writes); tx rollback simulation via $transaction.mockImplementationOnce wrapping try/catch — verifies the throw bubbles out and the service never returns; termination ACTIVE→TERMINATED/COMPLETED both flip AVAILABLE; 404 CONTRACT_NOT_FOUND; same-state no-op; re-activation 409 and RENTED-on-success; rollback when contract.update fails mid-tx (property.update never runs); RentalProcess.CLOSED with NEGOTIATING → AVAILABLE; CLOSED with RENTED leaves property alone; non-terminal transition does not release property. Verification: npx tsc --noEmit → exit 0; npx vitest run tests/rentalLifecycle.test.ts → 13/13 pass; related test set (rentalLifecycle + propertyCurrentTenant + propertyStatus + propertyPhotoUpload + seedUuidRegression + seed + generators + demoIds + visitController + visitService) → 111/111 pass; full suite 242 pass / 15 fail — same pre-existing Auth0→Firebase baseline (auth.test.ts, userSync.test.ts, ingestKnowledge.test.ts), +13 vs. US-004 (229/15), no regression. No DB migration needed (Contract + ContractStatus already exist).

userStories.title: PUT /api/properties/:id accepts multipart/form-data with photos[]
userStories.description: As a landlord, I want to add new photos during edit by sending multipart to the PUT endpoint — reusing the existing propertyPhotoUploadHandler from POST — without breaking JSON-only callers.
acceptanceCriteria: Route PUT /api/properties/:id accepts both Content-Type: application/json AND multipart/form-data; the multer middleware (propertyPhotoUploadHandler) runs only on the multipart branch

When req.files is present, propertyService update path saves them via the existing propertyImageStorageService.savePropertyImages and persists PropertyImage rows in the same transaction as the scalar updates
First new photo becomes isCover=true ONLY if the property has no existing cover; otherwise all new photos are saved with isCover=false (no silent cover replacement)
Authorization: only the landlord who owns the property can PUT — everyone else receives 403 FORBIDDEN in the existing { status, code, messages } shape
JSON-only PUT continues to work unchanged (regression test: PUT application/json with only scalar fields returns 200 and existing images are untouched)
Integration test: upload 2 photos via multipart on an existing property → GET returns 2 new image URLs persisted, existing images preserved
Integration test: non-owner attempting multipart PUT returns 403
Swagger JSDoc for PUT /properties/:id documents BOTH application/json and multipart/form-data content types, mirroring the pattern used on POST (US-009 of the prior ralph run)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implementation spans the full request→service stack: (1) new conditionalPropertyPhotoUploadHandler in src/middlewares/uploadMiddleware.ts — checks req.headers['content-type']; on 'multipart/form-data' it delegates to the existing propertyPhotoUploadHandler (multer.array('photos', 20)), otherwise next() so express.json() keeps parsing JSON bodies; (2) PUT route rewired in src/routes/propertyRoutes.ts with [...authStack, conditionalPropertyPhotoUploadHandler, propertyController.update] — authStack defined locally in propertyRoutes.ts because propertyRoutes is mounted WITHOUT authStack at app.ts (only touching PUT this story so existing POST/GET/DELETE paths stay unauthenticated at the Express layer, matching propertyPhotoUpload + propertyStatus + propertyCurrentTenant regression tests' assumptions); (3) controller.update adds a 401 guard on req.localUser and a pre-Zod ownership check: calls propertyService.getPropertyById(id) → 404 if missing / 403 FORBIDDEN if existing.landlordId !== localUser.id, only then Zod-parses body and forwards files to the service; (4) propertyService.updateProperty(id, data, files?) — when files is empty/undefined it's the legacy JSON path returning property.update with { include: images } (shape widened from `Property | null` to `PropertyWithImages | null`, unobservable in practice since the original JSON path callers didn't read images); when files are present, wraps tx { property.update, savePropertyImages, propertyImage.createMany } in a single prisma.$transaction and — critically — overrides savePropertyImages' default `isCover: i===0` in the createMany step: if `exists.images.some(img => img.isCover)` then all new rows get isCover=false (no silent cover replacement), else only the first gets isCover=true. Cleanup-on-rollback mirrors the existing createProperty pattern (cleanupPropertyImages catch-and-log, no re-throw). Swagger JSDoc for PUT /:id rewritten: documents BOTH content types, mirroring POST; adds `security: [{bearerAuth: []}]`, 401/403 responses, and the multipart photos field description with the cover rule. Tests: (a) new tests/propertyUpdateMultipart.test.ts with 6 cases — owner multipart PUT with 2 photos (service gets 2-element files array), owner JSON-only PUT preserves images, non-owner multipart returns 403 FORBIDDEN + service untouched, unauthenticated returns 401, 404 on missing property, 400 INVALID_FILE_TYPE when text/plain file attached; auth mocked via vi.mock('../src/middlewares/authMiddleware') with a header-driven switch ('Bearer landlord-owner' vs 'Bearer landlord-intruder') so the same app instance can exercise both the pass and fail branches of the ownership guard; (b) updated tests/propertyStatus.test.ts with the same authMiddleware mock (LANDLORD_ID hoisted into the mock factory) plus the `undefined` third arg on the toHaveBeenCalledWith assertion to account for the new files parameter. Verification: npx tsc --noEmit → exit 0; npx vitest run tests/propertyUpdateMultipart.test.ts → 6/6 pass; related set (propertyUpdateMultipart + propertyStatus + propertyPhotoUpload + propertyCurrentTenant + seedUuidRegression + rentalLifecycle + seed + generators + demoIds) → 85/85 pass; full suite 248 pass / 15 fail — same pre-existing Auth0→Firebase baseline, +6 vs US-005 (242/15), exactly the 6 new tests, no regression.

userStories.title: PUT /api/properties/:id handles photosToRemove[] multipart field
userStories.description: As a landlord, I want to delete specific photos during edit by sending their URLs in the repeated multipart field photosToRemove — processed before any new photos are added.
acceptanceCriteria: Multer is configured to accept the repeated multipart field `photosToRemove` (strings, not files) alongside `photos` files — parsed into req.body.photosToRemove as string[]
Each submitted URL is validated to belong to the property being edited — any URL that does not match returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR (no 404, to avoid leaking existence of other properties' photos)

Processing order inside the service: (1) validate photosToRemove URLs, (2) run a $transaction that deletes PropertyImage rows + scalar field updates + inserts for new photos, (3) after commit, delete actual files from storage via cleanupPropertyImages; storage delete failures are logged (warn) but do not reverse the DB commit
If the removed photo was isCover, the next remaining image (oldest by createdAt) is promoted to isCover=true in the same transaction
Integration test: upload 3 photos → PUT multipart with photosToRemove[] containing one existing URL → GET returns 2 photos, removed one gone, cover correctly reassigned if applicable
Integration test: PUT with photosToRemove[] containing a URL that belongs to ANOTHER property returns 400
Integration test: combined PUT (new photos + photosToRemove) → both take effect in one request
Swagger multipart schema for PUT includes `photosToRemove: { type: array, items: { type: string, format: uri } }`
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: No multer config change was needed — `.array('photos', 20)` already routes non-file fields into `req.body`, so a repeated `photosToRemove` multipart field lands as `req.body.photosToRemove` (string[] for 2+, string for 1). Normalization moved up to Zod: new `photosToRemoveField` in `src/utils/propertyValidation.ts` uses `z.preprocess` to lift a single string into an array and drops empty strings, then validates `z.array(z.string().min(1)).optional()`. Accepted as relative paths (the PropertyImage.url shape used in this repo is `/uploads/<id>/<file>.jpg`) — `z.string().url()` would have rejected the real data. New `PropertyError` class exported from `src/services/propertyService.ts` (same shape as ContractError/VisitError/ProposalError — `httpStatus, code, message`). `propertyService.updateProperty` now splits `photosToRemove` off the Zod-validated input before hitting `prisma.property.update`, then: (a) dedupes the URL list via `new Set`, (b) reads `exists.images` (already `include:images` from US-006) and validates every URL is in that set — any miss throws `PropertyError(400, 'VALIDATION_ERROR', ...)` BEFORE any tx opens (asserted in tests via `$transaction` spy count = 0), (c) inside `prisma.$transaction`: `propertyImage.deleteMany({ where: { propertyId, url: { in: photosToRemove } } })` — Prisma's AND on propertyId is defense-in-depth even though the belongs-to check already ran; then if the removed set contained the current cover AND at least one existing image remains, the oldest remaining by `createdAt` is promoted via `propertyImage.update({ data: { isCover: true } })`; then scalar update; then new-photo insert reusing the US-006 cover rule but sourcing `hasCoverAfterRemoval` from post-removal state (either the surviving cover, or the just-promoted one). Post-commit: `cleanupPropertyImages(id, photosToRemove)` is awaited outside the tx with `.catch(logger.warn)` so storage-side failures never roll back the DB commit. Controller `propertyController.update` adds `err instanceof PropertyError` → serialize to `{ status, code, messages }`; untouched by the files path of US-006. Swagger JSDoc for PUT /:id now documents `photosToRemove: { type: array, items: { type: string, format: uri } }` plus a top-level description of the cover-reassign side-effect and the 400-vs-404 rule. Tests: new `tests/propertyPhotosRemove.test.ts` (8 service-level cases, mocks `../src/config/db` + `../src/services/propertyImageStorageService`) covers (1) remove-one-of-three with cover promotion to oldest remaining, (2) cross-property URL → `PropertyError(400, VALIDATION_ERROR)` + zero tx calls + zero cleanup, (3) combined removal+new photos in a single tx with `hasCoverAfterRemoval=true` so new photos stay `isCover=false`, (4) cover removed + no existing remaining + one new photo → new photo promoted to cover, (5) dedupe of repeated URLs, (6) missing property returns null without opening a tx, (7) rollback path: scalar update throws → cleanup NOT called (the tx commit never happened), (8) PropertyError shape smoke test. Extended `tests/propertyUpdateMultipart.test.ts` with 4 HTTP-level cases: repeated `.field('photosToRemove')` arrives as string[] at the service; single `.field` is normalized to a one-element array; service-thrown PropertyError surfaces as 400 VALIDATION_ERROR in the standard `{ status, code, messages }` shape; combined multipart (photos + photosToRemove) reaches the service intact. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit` → exit 0; `npx vitest run tests/propertyPhotosRemove.test.ts tests/propertyUpdateMultipart.test.ts` → 18/18 pass; related set (propertyPhotosRemove + propertyUpdateMultipart + propertyStatus + propertyPhotoUpload + propertyCurrentTenant + seedUuidRegression + rentalLifecycle + seed + generators + demoIds) → 97/97 pass; full suite 260 pass / 15 fail — same pre-existing Auth0→Firebase baseline (auth.test.ts, userSync.test.ts, ingestKnowledge.test.ts), +12 vs end of US-006 (248/15), exactly the 8+4 new tests. No regression.

userStories.title: Add RentalPayment model + migration
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I need a RentalPayment table to persist monthly rent payment status per property-period.
acceptanceCriteria: prisma/schema.prisma adds model RentalPayment with fields: id (uuid default), propertyId (relation to Property), period (string, 'YYYY-MM' format), status (enum PaymentStatus: AWAITING|PAID|LATE, default AWAITING), updatedAt (DateTime), updatedBy (relation to User, nullable)
Unique constraint (propertyId, period) enforced in the schema
Index on propertyId for efficient per-property queries
Migration generated via prisma migrate dev --name add_rental_payment and committed
Migration applies cleanly on a fresh DB
Swagger components.schemas adds a RentalPayment schema (no route docs yet — that's US-009/US-010)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Semantic deviation documented: PRD says 'enum PaymentStatus: AWAITING|PAID|LATE' but the schema already defines a `PaymentStatus` enum (PENDING|PAID|OVERDUE|CANCELLED) owned by `TenantPayment` (contract billing cycle). Prisma enforces unique enum names per schema, so the new enum is named `RentalPaymentStatus` (AWAITING|PAID|LATE, default AWAITING) — same three values the PRD specifies, different name. The Prisma client-side type is `RentalPaymentStatus`, imported from `@prisma/client` in downstream US-009/US-010. Schema adds: `enum RentalPaymentStatus { AWAITING PAID LATE }`; `model RentalPayment { id @id @default(uuid), propertyId @map('property_id'), period String @db.VarChar(7) // YYYY-MM, status RentalPaymentStatus @default(AWAITING), updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt @map('updated_at'), updatedBy String? @map('updated_by'); property Property @relation(..., onDelete: Cascade); updatedByUser User? @relation('RentalPaymentUpdatedBy', ..., onDelete: SET NULL); @@unique([propertyId, period], name: 'rental_payments_property_period_key'); @@index([propertyId], name: 'rental_payments_property_idx'); @@map('rental_payments') }`. Inverse relations added on User (`rentalPaymentUpdates RentalPayment[] @relation('RentalPaymentUpdatedBy')`) and Property (`rentalPayments RentalPayment[]`). Migration hand-authored as `prisma/migrations/20260507160015_add_rental_payment/migration.sql` (per Codebase Patterns — DATABASE_URL points at the live Supabase pooler, so `prisma migrate dev` is not safe to run locally). The migration SQL was verified by `npx prisma migrate diff --from-empty --to-schema-datamodel prisma/schema.prisma --script` — the generated DDL for rental_payments matches the hand-authored file byte-for-byte (CREATE TYPE RentalPaymentStatus, CREATE TABLE rental_payments with VARCHAR(7) period + DEFAULT 'AWAITING', rental_payments_pkey, rental_payments_property_id_period_key UNIQUE INDEX, rental_payments_property_idx, CASCADE on property FK + SET NULL on user FK). Swagger `components.schemas.RentalPayment` added (required: propertyId, period, status; period pattern ^\d{4}-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])$; status enum AWAITING|PAID|LATE default AWAITING; updatedAt nullable date-time; updatedBy nullable uuid; description flags that the API returns the AWAITING default without persisting when no row exists). No route docs yet — that's US-009/US-010. Verification: `npx prisma validate` → valid; `npx prisma generate` → client regenerated with RentalPaymentStatus + RentalPayment types; `npx tsc --noEmit` → exit 0 (no callers yet but the client generation surfaces any type holes); full suite 260 pass / 15 fail — same pre-existing Auth0→Firebase baseline (auth.test.ts, userSync.test.ts, ingestKnowledge.test.ts), zero new tests (no business logic yet — US-009/US-010 add the endpoints and cover integration tests), no regression. userStories.title: GET /api/properties/:id/payments/current
userStories.description: As a landlord, I want to read the current month's rent payment status for my property — returning AWAITING by default when no record exists yet.
acceptanceCriteria: Route registered under src/routes/propertyRoutes.ts (or a new paymentRoutes.ts — pick the cleanest; document choice in commit)
Authorization: only the property owner (landlord) can read — non-owner gets 403
Period is computed server-side as `YYYY-MM` of the server's current date (not from the client)
When a RentalPayment row exists for (propertyId, period), return { period, status, updatedAt: ISO8601, updatedBy: uuid|null }
When no row exists, return the SAME shape with status='AWAITING' and updatedAt/updatedBy=null (NOT 404 — the UI relies on always getting a default)
Integration test: no row → response has status AWAITING and null updatedAt/updatedBy; row exists → response reflects stored values
Integration test: non-owner returns 403; unauthenticated returns 401
Swagger documents endpoint, path params, and response schema (referencing the RentalPayment component)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implementation split into service (testable in isolation), controller (ownership + auth), and route (authStack + Swagger). (1) `src/services/rentalPaymentService.ts` — new module exporting `currentPeriod(now = new Date())` which formats UTC year/month into `YYYY-MM` (zero-padded), plus `rentalPaymentService.getCurrent(propertyId, now?)` which does a single `prisma.rentalPayment.findUnique({ where: { rental_payments_property_period_key: { propertyId, period } }, select: { period, status, updatedAt, updatedBy } })` — the compound-unique key name follows the `@@unique(..., name: ...)` convention documented in US-008 Codebase Patterns (client-side key, not DB index name). When the row is absent, returns the default shape `{ period, status: 'AWAITING', updatedAt: null, updatedBy: null }` WITHOUT persisting — honoring the PRD contract that GET must never insert. When present, returns the same shape with `updatedAt.toISOString()`. (2) `src/controllers/rentalPaymentController.ts` — `getCurrent` handler mirrors `propertyController.update` ordering: 401 on missing `req.localUser`, then `propertyService.getPropertyById(id)` → 404 if null / 403 FORBIDDEN if `property.landlordId !== localUser.id`, only then `rentalPaymentService.getCurrent(id)` and 200 response. 404-before-403 matches the repo-wide pattern set by US-006. Decision documented at the top of progress.txt for this story: either (A) extend RentalProcess with `pdfUrl String?`, `signedAt DateTime?` and reuse startDate/endDate/monthlyValue if they already exist (preferred if fields overlap) OR (B) create a new Contract model with { id, propertyId, tenantId (nullable), startDate, endDate, monthlyValue, pdfUrl?, signedAt?, createdAt, updatedAt } and a 1:1 relation to RentalProcess

userStories.notes: Decision (Option C — documented at the top of progress.txt for this story): extend the EXISTING Contract model. Option A was infeasible — RentalProcess has no startDate/endDate/monthlyValue/landlordId columns, so reusing it would have required inventing them. Option B was redundant — a full Contract model already shipped in 20260504204911_add_contract_logic carrying id, propertyId, tenantId (NOT NULL), landlordId, startDate, endDate, monthlyRent, dueDay, contractUrl, status, createdAt, updatedAt; creating a parallel Contract alongside would have duplicated the same columns and orphaned the existing 'Property.status=RENTED when Contract.status=ACTIVE' invariant enforced by contractService (US-005). Option C keeps Contract as the single source of truth for 'active rental cycle'. Two deltas vs PRD literal contract: (1) tenantId stays NOT NULL (PRD suggested nullable); the codebase has always required a tenant on contract creation and every consumer (contractService.createContract signature, proposalService ACCEPTED branch, listTenantContracts) assumes non-null — relaxing it would be scope creep unrelated to the digital cycle US-015/US-016 need. (2) The PRD's 'monthlyValue' field is named monthlyRent in-repo; the DB column is monthly_rent — no rename, only the new 'pdfUrl/signedAt' pair. Changes: (a) schema.prisma Contract — renamed field contractUrl → pdfUrl (@map('pdf_url')), added signedAt DateTime? @map('signed_at'). ADD COLUMN signed_at TIMESTAMP(3) (NULL default — pre-existing rows correctly represent 'no signed PDF yet'). Authorization: caller must be the property's landlord OR the specified tenant; otherwise 403

userStories.notes: Decision docs: (a) PRD response field literal was `monthlyValue` but per US-013 the codebase standardized on `monthlyRent` (DB column monthly_rent, Prisma field monthlyRent, Swagger Contract component monthlyRent). Renaming just the response field for this one endpoint would have created a two-name-for-one-field inconsistency across the API surface — kept `monthlyRent` for consistency and documented the deviation here. (b) PRD said 'fetch the active contract' in the description; service filters `status='ACTIVE'` so TERMINATED/COMPLETED contracts correctly return 404 CONTRACT_NOT_FOUND even when (propertyId, tenantId) ids match. (c) Guard ordering mirrors US-012 conversations/resolve: 401 UNAUTHORIZED → 400 VALIDATION_ERROR (Zod) → 404 NOT_FOUND (property missing) → 403 FORBIDDEN (caller is neither landlord nor the queried tenant) → 404 CONTRACT_NOT_FOUND (no ACTIVE contract) → 200 projection. Property is loaded first so landlordId can be derived server-side for the authorization check WITHOUT exposing contract.landlordId to non-participants. Delivered: (1) src/utils/contractValidation.ts — new `getContractQuerySchema` (z.object with propertyId/tenantId uuids, both required). (2) src/services/contractService.ts — new `getActiveContractByPropertyAndTenant(propertyId, tenantId)` exporting a `ContractByPropertyTenantView` type: projects {id, propertyId, tenantId, landlordId, startDate (ISO), endDate (ISO), monthlyRent (number), pdfUrl (string|null), signedAt (ISO|null)}. landlordId is kept in the service return for the controller's auth check but stripped from the HTTP response. Uses `prisma.contract.findFirst({ where: { propertyId, tenantId, status:'ACTIVE' }, orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' } })` so the most recent ACTIVE contract wins if multiple exist. Decimal→number via `Number(row.monthlyRent)`; Date→ISO via `.toISOString()`; nullable pdfUrl/signedAt pass through as explicit null (not undefined — PRD requires field presence). (3) src/controllers/contractController.ts — new `getByPropertyAndTenant` handler with the 5-step guard flow above; on success, destructures `{ landlordId: _, ...view }` so landlordId is omitted from the JSON body. (4) src/routes/contractRoutes.ts — GET /contracts registered BEFORE GET /contracts/:id (path-segment count is different so Express ordering is not load-bearing, but lexical proximity aids readability); full Swagger JSDoc documents all 400/401/403/404 responses with ErrorResponse refs and explicitly calls out that `pdfUrl`/`signedAt` are nullable and populated only via US-016. Tests: (a) tests/contractByPropertyTenant.test.ts — 9 HTTP cases reusing the header-driven auth mock from conversationResolve.test.ts (Bearer landlord-owner / the-tenant / outsider + no-header): landlord+tenant read success with exact body shape assertion (including landlordId absence, explicit null pdfUrl/signedAt, populated pdfUrl/signedAt passthrough); 404 CONTRACT_NOT_FOUND when service returns null; 403 FORBIDDEN for outsider (service not called); 401 UNAUTHORIZED when anonymous; 400 VALIDATION_ERROR for non-uuid propertyId / tenantId / missing tenantId; 404 NOT_FOUND when propertyService returns null. (b) tests/contractService.test.ts — 4 service-level cases mocking `../src/config/db`: asserts exact where:{propertyId,tenantId,status:'ACTIVE'} shape; returns null when findFirst returns null; serializes pdfUrl/signedAt when populated and Decimal→number is the right primitive; orderBy:{createdAt:'desc'} is present. SPLIT INTO TWO FILES because a single file that mocks both `contractService` (at vi.mock top-level for HTTP tests) AND `db` (for service-level tests) would have the HTTP mock shadow the real service being tested — the service tests would exercise the mock instead. Verification: npx tsc --noEmit → exit 0; npx vitest run tests/contractByPropertyTenant.test.ts tests/contractService.test.ts → 13/13 pass; full suite 309 pass / 15 fail — same pre-existing Auth0→Firebase baseline (auth.test.ts, userSync.test.ts, ingestKnowledge.test.ts), +13 vs end of US-013 (296/15), exactly the 9+4 new tests. No regression. No DB migration (Contract model was already shipped in US-013).

userStories.title: GET /api/contracts/:id/pdf
userStories.description: As a landlord or tenant, I want to download the contract PDF via the API.
acceptanceCriteria: Route GET /api/contracts/:id/pdf
Authorization: caller must be a party to the contract (landlord OR tenant); otherwise 403

If the storage backend supports signed URLs, respond 302 with a short-lived signed URL in Location; otherwise stream the file with Content-Type: application/pdf. Document the choice at the top of progress.txt for this story
If the contract has no pdfUrl, return 404 code 'CONTRACT_PDF_NOT_AVAILABLE'

Tests pass
userStories.notes: Storage strategy decision: the repo ships with local filesystem storage (src/app.ts mounts `express.static('/uploads')` at line 47, propertyImageStorageService writes under `uploads/<propertyId>/...`) and has no cloud backend / signed-URL support in-tree. The endpoint therefore STREAMS the file with Content-Type: application/pdf when pdfUrl is relative (`/uploads/...`), AND 302-redirects when pdfUrl is absolute (http/https). The 302 branch is forward-compatible with a future S3/Firebase + signed-URL migration — a storage backend that produces absolute URLs will flow through without a controller change. Implementation: new `getContractDownloadContext(id)` in src/services/contractService.ts projects only {id, landlordId, tenantId, pdfUrl} (narrow select — no payments/property/tenant joins). Controller in src/controllers/contractController.ts#getPdf enforces guard order 401 → 404 NOT_FOUND → 403 FORBIDDEN → 404 CONTRACT_PDF_NOT_AVAILABLE (unified for null pdfUrl AND missing file on disk), redirects absolute URLs with Cache-Control: private, no-store, and for relative paths strips the leading slash, resolves against `path.resolve(__dirname, '../../', relative)`, and rejects paths that escape UPLOADS_ROOT (defense-in-depth against stored-value path traversal). Cache-Control is set explicitly; Content-Type is left to res.sendFile's extension-based mime lookup (setting it upfront would win against res.json() on the ENOENT branch and serve the error envelope as application/pdf — verified with a failing test first, then fixed). Route GET /contracts/:id/pdf registered in src/routes/contractRoutes.ts between GET /contracts/:id and PATCH /contracts/:id/status (distinct segment counts, no Express matching conflicts) with full Swagger JSDoc covering 200 application/pdf body, 302 Location header, and 401/403/404 via ErrorResponse ref. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit` → exit 0; `npx vitest run tests/contractPdf.test.ts tests/contractService.test.ts` → 16/16 pass (9 new HTTP cases + 3 new service cases on top of the existing 4 from US-014); `npx vitest run tests/contractPdf.test.ts tests/contractService.test.ts tests/contractByPropertyTenant.test.ts tests/rentalLifecycle.test.ts` → 38/38 pass; full suite: 321 pass / 15 fail — same pre-existing Auth0→Firebase baseline (auth.test.ts, userSync.test.ts, ingestKnowledge.test.ts), +12 vs. end of US-014 (309/15), exactly the 9+3 new tests, no regression. Tests write a real minimal `%PDF-1.4...` payload under uploads/<tmp>/ via mkdir+writeFile, assert first 4 bytes via a supertest custom parser buffering the stream, and also exercise absolute-URL 302, missing-file ENOENT, path-traversal rejection, null pdfUrl, 403, 401, and 404 NOT_FOUND. No DB migration (Contract.pdfUrl already shipped in US-013).

userStories.title: PUT /api/contracts/:id/signed-document (multipart)

acceptanceCriteria: Route PUT /api/contracts/:id/signed-document with multipart field signedPdf (single file)
Authorization: only the contract's landlord can upload; everyone else gets 403
Validation: accept ONLY application/pdf AND verify magic bytes (first 4 bytes == '%PDF') on the buffer; reject with 400 code 'INVALID_FILE_TYPE' otherwise

Response: { signedAt, pdfUrl }
Integration test: valid PDF upload → GET /api/contracts/:id returns signedAt and pdfUrl; follow-up GET /pdf returns the uploaded bytes
Integration test: magic-bytes mismatch → 400 INVALID_FILE_TYPE
Integration test: non-landlord → 403
Swagger documents endpoint with multipart schema, size limit, and response
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Size limit decision (per acceptance criteria — document the choice): 15MB default, configurable via env var CONTRACT_PDF_MAX_BYTES. Justification: signed-contract PDFs frequently exceed the 10MB photo cap (digital signature cert chains + multi-page scans), and a single env knob is cheaper than a code change if a specific landlord hits the cap. Zero-config default covers the common case; the env var is the escape hatch. Storage: reused the existing local-filesystem convention from propertyImageStorageService (US-006/US-007) — new parallel service src/services/contractDocumentStorageService.ts mirrors its save/cleanup shape but scopes disk layout under uploads/contracts/<contractId>/ to avoid namespace collision with property uploads. Implementation spans six new/updated files: (1) src/middlewares/contractDocumentUploadMiddleware.ts — new multer.single('signedPdf') config with memoryStorage, fileSize=CONTRACT_PDF_MAX_BYTES (15MB default), files=1, fileFilter accepting only 'application/pdf' (any other mime signals Error('INVALID_PDF_FILE_TYPE')); (2) src/middlewares/errorHandler.ts — added branch mapping Error('INVALID_PDF_FILE_TYPE') → {400, code:'INVALID_FILE_TYPE', message:'Apenas arquivos PDF são aceitos'}; the original 'INVALID_FILE_TYPE' branch for property images is untouched; (3) src/services/contractDocumentStorageService.ts — new service exports saveSignedContractPdf(contractId, buffer) → { url, absolutePath } writing under uploads/contracts/<contractId>/<randomUUID>.pdf + removeContractDocumentAbsolute(path) for rollback compensation + cleanupContractDocument(contractId, url) for URL-driven deletes (tolerates ENOENT and ENOTEMPTY rmdir like propertyImageStorageService); (4) src/services/contractService.ts — new SignedDocumentView type {pdfUrl, signedAt} + attachSignedPdfToContract(id, pdfUrl) which writes {pdfUrl, signedAt:new Date()} via prisma.contract.update with narrow select {pdfUrl, signedAt} and returns the ISO-serialized view. No prisma.$transaction here because the DB change is a single-row update — storage write is the other half of the couple and is compensated by outer try/catch in the controller; (5) src/controllers/contractController.ts — new uploadSignedDocument handler follows the US-015 guard order: 401 UNAUTHORIZED → 400 VALIDATION_ERROR (missing file) → 400 INVALID_FILE_TYPE (magic-bytes first 4 bytes != '%PDF') → 404 NOT_FOUND (contract missing) → 403 FORBIDDEN (caller != contract.landlordId — deliberately excluding tenant per PRD; only landlord can upload) → 200 {pdfUrl, signedAt}. Magic-bytes re-check is defense-in-depth even though multer's fileFilter already passes mime-type — a client can lie about Content-Type on a .pdf-named text file. Storage-first, DB-second ordering: if DB write fails, removeContractDocumentAbsolute(saved.absolutePath) runs inside the catch so disk and DB stay in sync; (6) src/routes/contractRoutes.ts — PUT /contracts/:id/signed-document registered BEFORE PATCH /contracts/:id/status with [contractDocumentUploadHandler, contractController.uploadSignedDocument] spread (auth is inherited from the global authStack at app.ts#118). userStories.title: POST /api/support/tickets
userStories.description: As a landlord or tenant, I want to open a support ticket via the app and receive a human-readable protocol code.
acceptanceCriteria: Route POST /api/support/tickets
Authentication: any authenticated role (JWT) — 401 if missing
Body validation via Zod: title (string, 1..120 chars), description (string, 1..4000 chars)
Server-side from JWT: userId, userName, userRole (TENANT|LANDLORD|ADMIN); createdAt auto

Generates `code` in format `SUP-AAMMDD-XXXX` where AAMMDD is today's date (server tz) and XXXX is 4 random chars in base36 uppercase — collision-safe (retry up to 5 times if unique violation, then bubble 500)
Response 201: { id, code, createdAt }
Calls supportEmailService.sendTicketCreated(ticket) after the DB insert — failures in the email path are logged but do NOT fail the API request
Integration test: POST returns 201 with `code` matching /^SUP-\d{6}-[A-Z0-9]{4}$/ and the row is persisted
Integration test: request without JWT → 401; title > 120 chars → 400; description > 4000 chars → 400
Swagger documents endpoint, body schema, and 201 response
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Endpoint ships as POST /api/support/tickets mounted under the global authStack in src/app.ts (new `app.use('/api', authStack, supportRoutes)` block after conversationRoutes). Delivered files: (1) src/utils/supportTicketValidation.ts — `createSupportTicketSchema = z.object({ title: z.string().min(1).max(120), description: z.string().min(1).max(4000) })`; title/description are the ONLY fields accepted from the body — `userId`, `userName`, `userRole`, `code`, `createdAt` are all server-derived. Extra fields submitted by the client (e.g., attempts to forge `userRole: 'ADMIN'`) are silently ignored by Zod default parse (not strict). (2) src/services/supportTicketService.ts — exports `generateTicketCode(now?)` (format `SUP-AAMMDD-XXXX` where AAMMDD uses local-timezone date to match what the support agent sees, and XXXX is 4 base36 uppercase chars from `Math.floor(Math.random() * 36**4).toString(36).toUpperCase().padStart(4, '0')`), `supportTicketService.create(author, payload, now?)` with P2002 retry loop (CODE_MAX_RETRIES=5), and `SupportTicketError` class mirroring the existing PropertyError/ContractError shape (httpStatus, code, message). On P2002 collision where `err.meta.target` includes 'code', regenerates and retries; any other error (different target, different P-code like P2003 FK violation) is rethrown immediately without retry. After 5 consecutive collisions (practical probability ~0 given 36^4 suffix space per day), throws SupportTicketError(500, 'CODE_GENERATION_FAILED'). (3) src/controllers/supportTicketController.ts — guard order 401 → Zod 400 → service → 201. Author derived from `req.localUser`: `{ id, name, role }` where role is mapped through `mapAuthorRole()` from the User.role enum (TENANT|LANDLORD|ADMIN) to SupportUserRole — identical value set, separate Prisma enum per the US-017 codebase-patterns rationale (actor-context captures use a dedicated enum so future Role splits don't retroactively mutate historical audit rows). Unknown future roles default to TENANT with a logger.warn rather than throwing. Response shape `{ id, code, createdAt: ISO }` matches the PRD contract exactly. supportEmailService.sendTicketCreated(ticket) is invoked with `void ...catch(logger.error)` AFTER the DB insert — the service already swallows transport failures (US-017 contract), and the outer .catch is defensive in case a synchronous throw bubbles through. SupportTicketError is caught in the controller's catch and serialized to `{ status, code, messages: [{message}] }`; all other errors bubble to next() → global errorHandler. (4) src/routes/supportRoutes.ts — registers `POST /support/tickets` with full Swagger JSDoc covering bearerAuth requirement, body schema (title/description min/max), 201 response with `{ id, code, createdAt }` (code regex `^SUP-\d{6}-[A-Z0-9]{4}$`), and 400/401 via ErrorResponse ref. (5) src/app.ts — new import + `app.use('/api', authStack, supportRoutes)` mount. Tests: (a) tests/supportTicketService.test.ts — 9 service-level cases mocking `../src/config/db`: generateTicketCode format assertion, local-date AAMMDD, 4-char base36 suffix with variance across 20 samples; create inserts with server-derived fields + code format + excluded-fields-left-default (status/resolution/assignedToId not in data); P2002 on `code` retries exactly twice and returns; 5 consecutive P2002 on `code` bubbles SupportTicketError(500, CODE_GENERATION_FAILED); P2003 FK violation is NOT retried (rethrown immediately); P2002 on a different column (e.g., `id`) is NOT retried; SupportTicketError instanceof Error with the expected shape. (b) tests/supportTicketCreate.test.ts — 13 HTTP cases using the same header-driven auth mock pattern as rentalPaymentCurrent/conversationResolve (Bearer the-tenant / the-landlord / the-admin with different localUser shapes): 201 + exact response shape for tenant; 201 for landlord with role LANDLORD captured; 201 for admin with role ADMIN captured; sendTicketCreated called exactly once AFTER insert with the full ticket; email rejection does NOT fail the request (still 201); 401 on missing Authorization with service untouched; 400 VALIDATION_ERROR for title>120; 400 for description>4000; 400 for missing title; 400 for missing description; 400 for empty title string; attempt-to-forge userId/userRole/code fields in body is dropped by Zod (service receives JWT-derived author, not body); SupportTicketError(500, 'CODE_GENERATION_FAILED') from service is surfaced as 500 with the right envelope. Swagger: the SupportTicket component schema was already shipped in US-017 (confirmed in src/config/swagger.ts with required/description/pattern/enums/nullable fields all aligned to this endpoint's contract), so no central-schema edit was needed — only the route-level JSDoc in supportRoutes.ts. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit` → exit 0; `npx vitest run tests/supportTicketCreate.test.ts tests/supportTicketService.test.ts` → 22/22 pass; related set (supportTicketCreate + supportTicketService + supportEmailService + conversationResolve + rentalPaymentCurrent) → 54/54 pass; full suite 364 pass / 15 fail — same pre-existing Auth0→Firebase baseline (auth.test.ts, userSync.test.ts, ingestKnowledge.test.ts), +22 vs end of US-017 (342/15), exactly the 9+13 new tests, no regression. No DB migration (SupportTicket model + indexes + FKs already shipped in US-017).

userStories.title: GET /api/admin/support/tickets (admin-only list + filters + pagination)
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to list and filter support tickets to triage them.
acceptanceCriteria: Route GET /api/admin/support/tickets — protected by JWT AND role=ADMIN (anything else returns 403, anon returns 401)
Query params (all optional): status (OPEN|RESOLVED), role (TENANT|LANDLORD), from (ISO date), to (ISO date), page (default 1, min 1), pageSize (default 50, min 1, max 200)
Response envelope: { data: Ticket[], page, pageSize, total }
Each Ticket item: { id, code, title, description, user: { id, name, email, role }, status, createdAt, updatedAt, assignedTo?: {id,name}, resolution? }
Default ordering: createdAt DESC
Integration test: admin lists all; non-admin authenticated → 403; unauthenticated → 401
Integration test: combined filters (status=RESOLVED&role=LANDLORD&from=...) return the correct subset; page/pageSize produce consistent envelope totals
Swagger documents endpoint, all query params, and paginated response envelope
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass
userStories.notes:
Admin-only list mounted as GET /api/admin/support/tickets alongside POST /api/support/tickets (US-018) in src/routes/supportRoutes.ts — adding it to supportRoutes kept all support-ticket HTTP surface in one file (vs. splitting into adminRoutes.ts which owns unrelated /admin/metrics + /admin/properties + /admin/broadcast). Route is gated by `requireRole('ADMIN')` — the existing middleware already returns the standard 403 FORBIDDEN envelope when role != ADMIN. Global authStack (checkJwt + authSyncMiddleware) is inherited from `app.use('/api', authStack, supportRoutes)` at app.ts#137, so 401 on missing/invalid token is handled upstream. Guard flow: 401 (missing Auth header) → 403 (non-admin) → 400 VALIDATION_ERROR (Zod on query) → 200 with envelope.

Delivered across five files: (1) src/utils/supportTicketValidation.ts — new `listSupportTicketsQuerySchema` with all optional filters (status: SupportTicketStatus enum, role: only TENANT|LANDLORD — ADMIN deliberately excluded from the triage filter per PRD, from/to: custom ISO-date-like validator via Date.parse since z.string().datetime() rejects the common YYYY-MM-DD calendar-picker form, page: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).default(1), pageSize: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).max(200).default(50)) + refine-level check that from <= to returns 400 if violated. z.coerce handles Express's string-only req.query shape; arrays fail the coercion and yield 400. (2) src/services/supportTicketService.ts — new `supportTicketService.list(params)` returning `ListSupportTicketsResult` envelope {data, page, pageSize, total}. Implementation uses `Promise.all([prisma.count({where}), prisma.findMany({where, orderBy:{createdAt:'desc'}, skip:(page-1)*pageSize, take:pageSize, include:{user:{select:{id,name,email,role}}, assignedTo:{select:{id,name}}}})])` — exactly 2 queries regardless of pageSize (no N+1; include is a single JOIN). Where clause is built conditionally: empty when no filters (returns all), createdAt is a gte/lte pair when either from or to is set. New exported types: `ListSupportTicketsParams`, `SupportTicketAdminView` (admin-facing row with `user: {id,name,email:string|null,role:string}|null, assignedTo: {id,name}|null, resolution: string|null, dates as ISO strings)`, `ListSupportTicketsResult`. `email` typed as `string | null` because User.email is `String?` in the schema — pre-existing Prisma-generated type. (3) src/controllers/supportTicketController.ts — new `listForAdmin` handler parses req.query via the Zod schema, converts from/to ISO strings to Date objects, forwards to the service, returns 200 JSON. ZodError falls through to the global errorHandler which emits the standard VALIDATION_ERROR envelope. (4) src/routes/supportRoutes.ts — imports `requireRole`, defines `const adminOnly = requireRole('ADMIN')`, registers `router.get('/admin/support/tickets', adminOnly, supportTicketController.listForAdmin)` with full Swagger JSDoc documenting all 6 query params, the 200 envelope schema inlined (with ticket item shape: id/code/title/description/user/status/createdAt/updatedAt/assignedTo?/resolution?), and 400/401/403 responses via ErrorResponse ref. (5) No DB migration — SupportTicket model + compound indexes (status,createdAt) and (userRole,createdAt) already shipped in US-017 and are exactly the indexes this list query needs.

Tests: (a) tests/adminSupportTicketsList.test.ts — 15 HTTP cases reusing the header-driven auth mock pattern from supportTicketCreate (Bearer the-tenant / the-landlord / the-admin with different localUser shapes). KEY DIFFERENCE vs prior mocks: this file uses `vi.mock('../src/middlewares/authMiddleware', async (importOriginal) => { const actual = await importOriginal(); return {...actual, checkJwt: stub, authSyncMiddleware: stub} })` — critically KEEPING the REAL `requireRole` function. That way 401 tests exercise the stubbed checkJwt branch, 403 tests exercise the REAL requireRole against a stubbed req.localUser with role: TENANT/LANDLORD. If requireRole were stubbed to always next(), the 403 tests would be testing nothing. Cases: admin success with full envelope + correct default service args; item shape assertion covering id/code regex/title/description/user{id,name,email,role}/status/assignedTo/resolution; combined filters status+role+from+to+page+pageSize forwarded with Date-converted from/to; defaults (page:1, pageSize:50); 403 for TENANT + zero service calls; 403 for LANDLORD + zero service calls; 401 anonymous + zero service calls; 400 VALIDATION_ERROR for status='INVALID'; 400 for role='ADMIN'; 400 for page=0; 400 for pageSize=201; 400 for from>to; 400 for from='not-a-date'; 200 for YYYY-MM-DD date format (common calendar picker output); total reflects DB count not page size. (b) tests/supportTicketService.test.ts extended with 8 new cases on the list method: envelope shape with ISO-serialized dates; orderBy:{createdAt:'desc'} + skip/take computed from page/pageSize; include with user{id,name,email,role}+assignedTo{id,name} (single JOIN, 2 total queries = no N+1); combined where {status, userRole, createdAt:{gte,lte}} shared by count and findMany so total reflects filters; empty where when no filters; createdAt.gte only when only from is provided; assignedTo populated correctly; null email pass-through for the schema's String? column.

Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit` → exit 0; `npx vitest run tests/adminSupportTicketsList.test.ts tests/supportTicketService.test.ts` → 32/32 pass (15+9 new + 8 existing from US-018); related support set (adminSupportTicketsList + supportTicketService + supportTicketCreate + supportEmailService) → 54/54 pass; full suite 387 pass / 15 fail — same pre-existing Auth0→Firebase baseline (auth.test.ts, userSync.test.ts, ingestKnowledge.test.ts), +23 vs end of US-018 (364/15), exactly the 15+8 new tests, no regression.

userStories.title: PUT /api/admin/support/tickets/:id (admin-only update)
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to update a ticket's status, resolution, and assignee when responding.
acceptanceCriteria: Route PUT /api/admin/support/tickets/:id — JWT + role=ADMIN only
Body (all optional but at least one required): { status?: 'OPEN'|'RESOLVED', resolution?: string (1..4000), assignedToId?: uuid }
Business rule: when status transitions to RESOLVED, resolution is required in the same request — 400 VALIDATION_ERROR if missing
If assignedToId is provided, the referenced User must exist — 400 VALIDATION_ERROR with code 'ASSIGNEE_NOT_FOUND' otherwise (do NOT 500 on FK violation)
On successful status change, invoke supportEmailService.sendTicketUpdated(ticket); failures are logged, not fatal
Response: full updated object in the same shape as a GET envelope item (from US-019)
Integration test: admin resolves ticket with resolution → GET returns RESOLVED with resolution; non-admin → 403
Integration test: status=RESOLVED without resolution → 400
Integration test: assignedToId for a non-existent user → 400 ASSIGNEE_NOT_FOUND
Swagger documents endpoint, body (including the RESOLVED requires-resolution rule as a description), and response
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass
userStories.notes:
Implemented PUT /api/admin/support/tickets/:id alongside POST /support/tickets (US-018) and GET /admin/support/tickets (US-019) in src/routes/supportRoutes.ts — `router.put('/admin/support/tickets/:id', adminOnly, supportTicketController.updateForAdmin)`. Global authStack (checkJwt + authSyncMiddleware) is inherited from app.ts#137; `adminOnly = requireRole('ADMIN')` gate is applied inline. Guard flow: 401 → 403 → 400 VALIDATION_ERROR (path id, body schema) → 404 TICKET_NOT_FOUND → 400 ASSIGNEE_NOT_FOUND → 200 with SupportTicketAdminView.

Delivered across four files plus 2 test files:
(1) src/utils/supportTicketValidation.ts — new `updateSupportTicketSchema` with three optional fields (status: SupportTicketStatus enum, resolution: z.string().min(1).max(4000), assignedToId: z.string().uuid()) + two refines: (a) at least one field required, (b) `status=RESOLVED` requires `resolution` in the same request. Zod emits a normal VALIDATION_ERROR envelope via the global errorHandler branch.
(2) src/services/supportTicketService.ts — new `supportTicketService.updateForAdmin(id, payload)` method. (a) findUnique pre-check → 404 TICKET_NOT_FOUND. (b) If `assignedToId` is provided AND differs from the existing value, prisma.user.findUnique → 400 ASSIGNEE_NOT_FOUND on null (beats the P2003 FK violation that Prisma would otherwise raise as 500). (c) Builds a minimal Prisma.SupportTicketUpdateInput that only contains fields actually provided (no spurious writes). assignedTo uses `connect: { id }` relation syntax. (d) Single prisma.supportTicket.update with include:{user{id,name,email,role}, assignedTo{id,name}} returns the row with the same shape as the GET list item. (a) path param `id` validated via `z.string().uuid().safeParse(req.params.id)` — returns a custom 400 envelope with path: 'id' when it fails (before touching the body). (b) body validated via updateSupportTicketSchema.parse. (c) `statusChanging = payload.status !== undefined` captured BEFORE the service call. (d) Service call → SupportTicketAdminView. (e) If status was touched, fires `supportEmailService.sendTicketUpdated(ticket)` in void-catch best-effort mode (failures logged, not fatal) — synthesized minimal SupportTicket-shaped object from the view since the view omits userId/userRole; the email service only reads code/status/userName/userRole/resolution. (f) Catches SupportTicketError → maps httpStatus/code/messages, otherwise next(err).
(4) src/routes/supportRoutes.ts — new PUT /admin/support/tickets/:id route with full Swagger JSDoc: path param uuid, body schema (status|resolution|assignedToId), description explaining the RESOLVED+resolution rule + the 400 ASSIGNEE_NOT_FOUND branch, 200/400/401/403/404 responses all via ErrorResponse ref (200 inlines the ticket shape matching US-019 items).

No DB migration — SupportTicket model already has all the columns needed (status, resolution, assignedToId) and the FK on assignedToId with onDelete:SetNull is exactly what this endpoint needs.

Tests: (a) tests/adminSupportTicketsUpdate.test.ts — 16 HTTP cases reusing the header-driven auth mock pattern from adminSupportTicketsList. KEY: preserves REAL `requireRole` via `importOriginal` spread so the 403 case actually exercises the real middleware against a stubbed req.localUser with role TENANT/LANDLORD. Cases cover: admin resolves ticket with resolution → 200 RESOLVED; admin updates resolution only (no status change) → 200 + NO email; admin assigns ticket → 200 with assignedTo populated; reopen RESOLVED → OPEN without resolution → 200; successful status change invokes sendTicketUpdated once; email failure is non-fatal (still 200); 400 for status=RESOLVED without resolution; 400 for empty body; 400 for invalid status enum; 400 for non-UUID assignedToId; 400 for empty resolution string; 400 for non-UUID path id; 400 ASSIGNEE_NOT_FOUND forwarded from service; 404 TICKET_NOT_FOUND forwarded from service; 403 for TENANT/LANDLORD; 401 for missing auth header.
(b) tests/supportTicketService.test.ts extended with 7 new cases on updateForAdmin: 404 TICKET_NOT_FOUND when findUnique returns null (update NOT called); 400 ASSIGNEE_NOT_FOUND when prisma.user.findUnique returns null (update NOT called); skips the user lookup when assignedToId equals current value (no-change short-circuit); updates status+resolution returning the view shape with ISO dates; prisma.update is called with only the provided fields (no spurious writes); assignedTo uses connect:{id} relation syntax; response carries ISO-serialized dates.

Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit` → exit 0; `npx vitest run tests/adminSupportTicketsUpdate.test.ts tests/supportTicketService.test.ts` → 41/41 pass (16+9 from this story + 16 existing US-018/US-019); full suite 411 pass / 15 fail — same pre-existing Auth0→Firebase baseline (auth.test.ts, userSync.test.ts, ingestKnowledge.test.ts), +24 vs end of US-019 (387/15), exactly the 16+8 new tests, no regression.
